description: Reference data for TFF Training Plan Skill. Question sequence is defined in SKILL.md — this file contains options, formulas and validation details only.
hr_zones_source_options: Yes — lab test (spiroergometry / lactate threshold test)
Yes — field test (20-min max effort test)
Yes — device estimate (Garmin, Polar, etc.) — note: may be off by 10-20 bpm
No — calculate from my age and resting HR

karvonen.formula: Zone bpm = Resting HR + (% × (Max HR - Resting HR))

examples._note: These examples illustrate correct assumption-stating behavior and output constraints. Reference these when applying Rule 9 (assumption transparency) and the complementary-declined case.

running_partial_info_pace_unknown.scenario: User provides goal, age, days, and duration but does not mention current 5k time or pace.
running_partial_info_pace_unknown.correct_behavior: State the assumption explicitly before proceeding. Do NOT silently estimate. Example output: 'You didn't mention your current pace — based on your intermediate fitness level, I'll estimate your easy pace at ~7:00/km (from the assessment reference table). I'll note this in the plan and you should adjust after your first session if it feels off.' Then continue with the next block.

strength_complementary_declined.scenario: User explicitly declines complementary cardio when asked in Block 5.
strength_complementary_declined.correct_behavior: Accept the answer and proceed to plan generation. At the very end of the completed plan (after all weekly sessions are shown), add exactly ONE line: 'Note: Adding 10 min of calf and hip stability work per week significantly reduces injury risk — recommended if you change your mind.' Do NOT add any other references to complementary training, cardio sessions, or injury prevention notes anywhere else in the plan body.

pace_estimation_when_unknown._instruction: Use these ranges when user skips pace or has no race data. Always state the estimate clearly. NEVER estimate above the max_easy_pace_cap — anything slower is walking, not running.

beginner.note: Walk-run intervals likely needed. Use lower end of range if user reports running continuously.

intermediate.note: Running continuously, building aerobic base. NEVER estimate above 7:30/km for intermediate — that is beginner territory.

advanced.note: Consistent training history, race experience likely.
